questions.questionText:
Statement-1 : If the rectangular components of a force are 8 N and 6 N, then the magnitude of the force is 10 N.

Statement-2 : If $|\vec{A}| = |\vec{B}| = 1$ then $|\vec{A} \times \vec{B}|^2 + |\vec{A} \cdot \vec{B}|^2 = 1$.
options: Statement-1 is True, Statement-2 is True; Statement-2 is a correct explanation for Statement-1.
Statement-1 is True, Statement-2 is True; Statement-2 is NOT a correct explanation for Statement-1
questions.subject: Physics

questions.questionText:
For component of a vector $\vec{A} = (3\hat{i} + 4\hat{j} - 5\hat{k})$, match the following table

Table 1 Table 2
(A) y-axis (P) 5 unit
(B) Along another vector $(2\hat{i} + \hat{j} + 2\hat{k})$ (Q) 4 unit
(C) Along another vector $(6\hat{i} + 8\hat{j} - 10\hat{k})$ (R) Zero
(D) Along another vector $(-3\hat{i} - 4\hat{j} + 5\hat{k})$ (S) None

Match the correct pairs from Table 1 and Table 2.
questions.subject: Physics

questions.questionText:
Statement-1: The angle between the two vectors $(\hat{i} + \hat{j})$ and $(\hat{k})$ is $\frac{\pi}{2}$ radian.

Statement-2: Angle between two vectors $(\hat{i} + \hat{j})$ and $(\hat{k})$ is given by $\theta = \cos^{-1}\left(\frac{\vec{A} \cdot \vec{B}}{AB}\right)$.
options: Statement-1 is True, Statement-2 is True; Statement-2 is a correct explanation for Statement-1.
Statement-1 is True, Statement-2 is True; Statement-2 is NOT a correct explanation for Statement-1.
questions.subject: Physics

questions.questionText: A plane is inclined at an angle of $30^{\circ}$ with horizontal. The component of a vector $\vec{A} = -10 \hat{k}$ perpendicular to this plane is (here z-direction is vertically upwards)
questions.subject: Physics

questions.questionText: The sum, difference and cross product of two vectors $\vec{A}$ and $\vec{B}$ are mutually perpendicular if
options: $\vec{A}$ and $\vec{B}$ are perpendicular to each other and $|\vec{A}| = |\vec{B}|$
$\vec{A}$ and $\vec{B}$ are perpendicular to each other
$\vec{A}$ and $\vec{B}$ are perpendicular but their magnitudes are arbitrary
questions.subject: Physics

questions.questionText: If $\vec{A}$, $\vec{B}$ and $\vec{C}$ are the unit vectors along the incident ray, reflected ray and the outward normal to the reflector. Then
questions.subject: Physics

questions.questionText:
Statement-1 : If three non-zero vectors $ \vec{A}, \vec{B} $ and $ \vec{C} $ satisfy the relation $ \vec{A} \cdot \vec{B} = 0 $ & $ \vec{A} \cdot \vec{C} = 0 $ then the vector $ \vec{A} $ is parallel to $ \vec{B} \times \vec{C} $.

Statement-2 : $ \vec{A} \perp \vec{B} $ and $ \vec{A} \perp \vec{C} $ and $ \vec{B} \times \vec{C} \neq 0 $ hence $ \vec{A} $ is perpendicular to plane formed by $ \vec{B} $ and $ \vec{C} $
options: Statement-1 is True, Statement-2 is True; Statement-2 is a correct explanation for Statement-1.
Statement-1 is True, Statement-2 is True; Statement-2 is NOT a correct explanation for Statement-1
questions.subject: Physics

questions.questionText: $ \vec{A} $ and $ \vec{B} $ are two vectors and $ \theta $ is the angle between them. If $ |\vec{A} \times \vec{B}| = \sqrt{3}(\vec{A} \cdot \vec{B}) $ then the value of $ \theta $ and $ |\vec{A} + \vec{B}| $ are :
questions.subject: Physics

questions.questionText:
If $\vec{a} = 2\hat{i} + 3\hat{j} + 6\hat{k}$ and $\vec{b} = 3\hat{i} + 4\hat{j}$, then

$$\frac{\text{projection of } \vec{a} \text{ on } \vec{b}}{\text{projection of } \vec{b} \text{ on } \vec{a}} =$$
questions.subject: Physics

questions.questionText: Two vector $\overrightarrow{\mathbf{A}}=2 \hat{\mathbf{i}}+3 \hat{\mathbf{j}}-4 \hat{\mathbf{k}}$ and $\overrightarrow{\mathbf{B}}=4 \hat{\mathbf{i}}+8 \hat{\mathbf{j}}+x \hat{\mathbf{k}}$ are such that the component of $\overrightarrow{\mathbf{B}}$ along $\overrightarrow{\mathbf{A}}$ is zero. Then the value of $x$ will be :
questions.subject: Physics

questions.questionText:
Find the volume of parallelepiped (in $\mathrm{m}^3$ ) whose edges are represented by

$$ a=(2 \hat{i}-3 \hat{j}+4 \hat{k}) \mathrm{m}, b=(\hat{i}+2 \hat{j}-\hat{k}) \mathrm{m} $$

and $c=(3 \hat{i}-\hat{j}+2 \hat{k}) \mathrm{m}$.
questions.subject: Physics

questions.questionText: If $a=2 \hat{i}-\hat{j}+\hat{k}, b=\hat{i}+2 \hat{j}-3 \hat{k}$ and $c=3 \hat{i}-y \hat{j}+5 \hat{k}$ are coplanar. Find the value of $y$.
questions.subject: Physics

questions.questionText: If none of the vectors $\vec{A}, \vec{B}$ and $\vec{C}$ are zero and if $\vec{A} \times \vec{B}=0$ and $\vec{B} \times \vec{C}=0$, then the value of $|\vec{A} \times \vec{C}|$ is _______
questions.subject: Physics

questions.questionText: Vectors $\vec{P}, \vec{Q}$ and $\vec{R}$ have magnitudes 5, 12, and 13 units and $\vec{P}+\vec{Q}=\vec{R}$. If angle between $\vec{Q}$ and $\vec{R}$ is $\theta$. Then find $\frac{13}{2} \cos \theta$.
questions.subject: Physics
